data.name: Action.Accept
data.value: Accept

data.name: Action.Cancel
data.value: Cancel

data.name: Action.Commit
data.value: Commit

data.name: Action.Copy
data.value: Copy

data.name: Action.EditBody
data.value: Edit body

data.name: Action.EditTitle
data.value: Edit title

data.name: Action.Regenerate
data.value: Regenerate

data.name: BranchAi.Title
data.value: Branch suggestion

data.name: Banner.Tagline
data.value: Git assistant

data.name: CommitAi.Committed
data.value: Commit created successfully.

data.name: CommitAi.CommitPrompt
data.value: Create the Git commit with this message?

data.name: CommitAi.CopyFailed
data.value: Unable to copy the commit message to the clipboard.

data.name: CommitAi.Copied
data.value: Commit message copied to the clipboard.

data.name: CommitAi.GeneratedByAi
data.value: Generated with AI

data.name: CommitAi.GeneratedByFallback
data.value: Generated with deterministic fallback

data.name: CommitAi.MixedConcerns
data.value: Your changes appear to mix more than one concern. Consider splitting them before committing.

data.name: CommitAi.NoChanges
data.value: No relevant changes were found for commit generation.

data.name: CommitAi.Title
data.value: AI commit

data.name: DangerousCommand.Confirm
data.value: Do you want to run this command anyway?

data.name: DangerousCommand.Title
data.value: Dangerous command

data.name: Doctor.Healthy
data.value: The repository looks healthy.

data.name: Doctor.Title
data.value: Repository doctor

data.name: Explain.Title
data.value: Command explanation

data.name: Explain.Label.Branch
data.value: Branch

data.name: Explain.Label.Head
data.value: HEAD

data.name: Explain.Label.Index
data.value: Index

data.name: Explain.Label.Notes
data.value: Notes

data.name: Explain.Label.Risk
data.value: Risk

data.name: Explain.Label.Summary
data.value: Summary

data.name: Explain.Label.Undo
data.value: Undo

data.name: Explain.Label.WorkingTree
data.value: Working tree

data.name: Explain.Empty.Summary
data.value: The command could not be parsed.

data.name: Explain.Empty.Unknown
data.value: Unknown.

data.name: Explain.Empty.Undo
data.value: Review the command syntax before running it.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Summary.Hard
data.value: Moves HEAD and the current branch, resets the index and discards working tree changes.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Summary.Default
data.value: Moves HEAD and may realign the index depending on the chosen mode.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Head
data.value: HEAD moves to the target commit.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Index.Hard
data.value: The index is reset to match the target commit.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Index.Default
data.value: The index may be reset to match the target commit.

data.name: Explain.Reset.WorkingTree.Hard
data.value: Local tracked changes in the working tree are discarded.

data.name: Explain.Reset.WorkingTree.Default
data.value: The working tree is preserved unless you chose a hard reset.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Branch
data.value: The current branch reference is rewritten to the target commit.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Undo
data.value: You can often recover the previous HEAD through git reflog, but uncommitted working tree changes dropped by --hard are not guaranteed to come back.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Note.Destructive
data.value: A hard reset is destructive for tracked changes.

data.name: Explain.Reset.Note.Snapshot
data.value: Create a safety snapshot before resetting if you are unsure.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Summary
data.value: Removes untracked files and directories from the working tree.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Head
data.value: HEAD does not move.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Index
data.value: The index is not changed.

data.name: Explain.Clean.WorkingTree
data.value: Untracked files can be permanently deleted depending on the flags used.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Branch
data.value: No branch reference changes.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Undo
data.value: There is no reliable undo for git clean unless the files are backed up elsewhere.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Note.Preview
data.value: Use git clean -nd first to preview what would be removed.

data.name: Explain.Clean.Note.Recovery
data.value: Untracked files are outside Git history, so recovery is limited.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Summary.Create
data.value: Creates or switches branches and updates the working tree to match the target.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Summary.Switch
data.value: Switches the current branch or restores paths depending on the arguments.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Head
data.value: HEAD may move to another commit or branch tip.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Index
data.value: The index is refreshed to match the checked out content.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.WorkingTree
data.value: Tracked files may be overwritten if the target branch conflicts with local changes.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Branch.Create
data.value: A new branch can be created and checked out.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Branch.Switch
data.value: The current branch may change.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Undo
data.value: If local changes were blocked or stashed, restore them from the stash. Branch switches are usually reversible by switching back.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Note.DirtyTree
data.value: Switching with a dirty working tree can fail or produce conflicts.

data.name: Explain.Checkout.Note.PreferSwitch
data.value: Prefer git switch for branch changes and git restore for file restores when possible.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Summary
data.value: Replays commits on top of another base, rewriting commit identities in the process.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Head
data.value: HEAD is moved as commits are rewritten onto the new base.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Index
data.value: The index is updated step by step during the replay.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.WorkingTree
data.value: Conflicts may stop the operation and leave temporary rebase state in the working tree.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Branch
data.value: The current branch history is rewritten.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Undo
data.value: Use git rebase --abort while the rebase is in progress. After completion, git reflog can often restore the previous branch tip.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Note.SharedHistory
data.value: Avoid rebasing shared public history unless your team expects it.

data.name: Explain.Rebase.Note.Snapshot
data.value: A snapshot before rebase makes recovery much safer.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Summary
data.value: Combines another branch into the current branch and may create a merge commit.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Head
data.value: HEAD stays on the current branch and advances to the merge result.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Index
data.value: The index stages merge results, including conflict markers when necessary.

data.name: Explain.Merge.WorkingTree
data.value: Conflicts may leave files in an unmerged state until resolved.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Branch
data.value: The current branch gains the merged history.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Undo
data.value: Use git merge --abort if the merge is still in progress. If the merge commit was created, git reset or reflog can move back.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Note.FastForward
data.value: Fast-forward merges move the branch without creating a merge commit.

data.name: Explain.Merge.Note.DirtyTree
data.value: Dirty working trees make merges riskier.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Summary
data.value: Creates a new commit from the current index.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Head
data.value: HEAD advances to a new commit on the current branch.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Index
data.value: Staged content becomes part of the new commit.

data.name: Explain.Commit.WorkingTree
data.value: Unstaged changes remain in the working tree.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Branch
data.value: The current branch moves to the new commit.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Undo
data.value: Use git reset --soft HEAD~1 to undo the commit while keeping changes staged.

data.name: Explain.Commit.Note.StagedOnly
data.value: Only staged changes are committed unless you use flags like -a.

data.name: Explain.Add.Summary
data.value: Stages content from the working tree into the index.

data.name: Explain.Add.Head
data.value: HEAD does not move.

data.name: Explain.Add.Index
data.value: Selected changes are added to the index.

data.name: Explain.Add.WorkingTree
data.value: The working tree keeps the same file contents.

data.name: Explain.Add.Branch
data.value: No branch reference changes.

data.name: Explain.Add.Undo
data.value: Use git restore --staged <path> to unstage changes.

data.name: Explain.Add.Note.Staging
data.value: Staging controls what will go into the next commit.

data.name: Explain.Status.Summary
data.value: Shows the relationship between HEAD, the index and the working tree.

data.name: Explain.Status.Head
data.value: HEAD does not move.

data.name: Explain.Status.Index
data.value: The index is not changed.

data.name: Explain.Status.WorkingTree
data.value: The working tree is not changed.

data.name: Explain.Status.Branch
data.value: No branch reference changes.

data.name: Explain.Status.Undo
data.value: No undo is needed because status is read-only.

data.name: Explain.Status.Note.Safe
data.value: This is a safe inspection command.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Summary
data.value: Shows differences between two Git states such as HEAD, index and working tree.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Head
data.value: HEAD does not move.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Index
data.value: The index is not changed.

data.name: Explain.Diff.WorkingTree
data.value: The working tree is not changed.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Branch
data.value: No branch reference changes.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Undo
data.value: No undo is needed because diff is read-only.

data.name: Explain.Diff.Note.Cached
data.value: Use --cached to inspect staged changes.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Summary
data.value: Explains the high-level impact of git {0}, but the exact behavior still depends on the flags and arguments you pass.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Head
data.value: HEAD impact depends on the subcommand options.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Index
data.value: Index impact depends on the subcommand options.

data.name: Explain.Generic.WorkingTree
data.value: Working tree impact depends on the subcommand options.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Branch
data.value: Branch impact depends on the subcommand options.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Undo
data.value: Check the exact flags before executing a potentially destructive variation of this command.

data.name: Explain.Generic.Note.Preview
data.value: For destructive variants, prefer previewing the command first.

data.name: General.Error
data.value: Error

data.name: General.None
data.value: None

data.name: General.Success
data.value: Success

data.name: General.Warning
data.value: Warning

data.name: Language.Detected
data.value: Language detected: {0}

data.name: Model.Missing
data.value: The configured model is unavailable. Install it locally and try again.

data.name: PrSummary.Title
data.value: PR summary

data.name: Recover.NoPoints
data.value: No recovery points were found for this repository.

data.name: Recover.Restored
data.value: Recovery point restored.

data.name: Recover.RestorePrompt
data.value: Restore this recovery point?

data.name: Recover.Title
data.value: Recovery

data.name: Snapshot.Created
data.value: Snapshot created: {0}

data.name: Snapshot.Title
data.value: Snapshot

data.name: Summarize.Title
data.value: Work summary

data.name: Why.Title
data.value: Why did this file change?

data.name: Risk.None
data.value: None

data.name: Risk.Low
data.value: Low

data.name: Risk.Medium
data.value: Medium

data.name: Risk.High
data.value: High

data.name: Risk.Critical
data.value: Critical
